sections.paragraph:
μαζός, οῦ, ὁ (Hom. et al.; Kaibel 644, 4; 690, 2; PSI 253, 134; s. Schwyzer I 472; collateral forms μασθός and μαστός [q.v.]) ‘one of the breasts’ (distinguished from the στῆθος ‘chest’, the area of the torso where the μαζοί are located; the distinction noted Il. 4, 480f βάλε στῆθος παρὰ μαζὸν δεξίον=he smote him in the chest near his right nipple).

① one of the mammillae of a male, nipple (oft. Hom.; Apollon. Rhod. 3, 119; Achilles Tat. 3, 8, 6; Etym. Mag. *574, 220) of the triumphant Christ Rv 1:13 v.l.

② mammary gland of a female, mamma, breast (Hom. et al.; Artem. 1, 16; TestNapht 1:12 v.l.; also of an animal’s udder: Callim. 1, 48; Aratus, Phaen. 163; Crinagoras no. 26, 6). Fig. (Philo, Aet. M. 66) of springs (Pampretius of Panopolis [V a.d.] 1, 90 [ed. HGerstinger: SBWienAk 208, 3, 1928]) which offer to humans τοὺς πρὸς ζωῆς μαζούς their life-giving breasts 1 Cl 20:10.—DELG s.v. μαστός. M-M.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

Schwyzer Schwyzer = ES., Griechische Grammatik—List 6

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

Il. Il. = Iliad, s. Hom.—List 5

oft. oft. = often

Achilles Tat Achilles Tat , IV a.d.—List 5

Mag.

Mag. = De Magistratibus Populi Romani

mag. = magic(al)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

TestNapht TestNapht = Testament of Naphthali, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Callim Callim , III b.c.—List 5

Crinagoras Crinagoras , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6
